item.title: Liz Lemon - Cosplay
item.description:
I didn’t share enough of these pics when I did this cosplay - Since I still get told I look like Tina Fey on here all the time when I go brunette (which, twist: per my last post you may notice is my real hair color :P) and I still just get a kick out of 30 Rock - here is me crushing it as Liz… Sabor De Soledad included :D
References I used :D
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Dumpin' memes and closin' tabs pt. 4 ft.
item.description:
Gentle reminder to take your meds and hydrate. Let's break this down, Barbara. My meds change me. I think they change me for the better, but let's put that aside for the moment. You know what else changes me? Vitamin C changes me from someone with scurvy into someone whose teeth aren't falling out. Sunlight changes me from a pale, depressed person into a less-pale, optimistic person. Damn near everything changes us in one way or another, and saying that the unmedicated me isn't the Real Me is a BOLD position to take. I - look at me, Babs - I, and only I get to decide what the real me is. Not you, or anyone else who needs xanax and white wine to get through 9 am. Not anyone trying to enforce any kind of phobic social norms. I don't want any part of the Real Me that conforms to THEIR standards. And if this is the Real You that I'm seeing in front of me, then I don't want any part of you, either. Good day to you, madam!
The cutlery is the easiest part, imo. You don't have to be as gentle with it because metal is more tolerant than ceramic or glass (IN THIS CONTEXT YOU NERDS)
I've taught a fair share of people how to play. When they win one on their own merit, that's cause for celebration, not regret.
Make yourself a wish list so you can have a ready answer for people when they ask what you want as a gift. Go through your older unread books with a critical eye and donate a few to a local library or make a little Free Library stand for your community.
This is not a grenade launcher. It's an anti-tank missile. Know your explosives, kids! It can save your life.
I said in my previous dump that I missed this wibbledy-wobbledy, and it popped up that very same day! HOW FORTUITOUS!
Everybody's crazy. There's more and less crazy, and there's good and bad crazy. The key to life is finding people whose craziness meshes well-enough with your own that you don't kill each other. Then try to keep them alive and healthy as long and as well as you can. One part of this is to make sure they take their meds and hydrate!
It could be an animated series like "What If" except titled "Meanwhile." We can get Stephen Colbert to do the intro like he does on the Late Show. "Folks, if you keep up with the Marvel Cinematic Universe, you know that most of the time I'm over there, showing you the Avengers facing off against existential threats and mortal enemies like Ultron, beings of power incomprehensible to mere mortals like you and I, with the power to eradicate human life across the globe, reshaping the future of our entire planet for all time to come, written by true fans of the comics both new and old, portrayed by Hollywood's most beloved actors, and lovingly rendered by hundreds of visual effects artists. But sometimes, just sometimes, folks, I come over here to bring you the ramshackle 5-minute, bite-sized story of Peter Parker and Ned - I wanna say Flanders? - asking Steve Rogers and James Buchanon "Bucky" Barnes for help studying for their AP History test and not learning anything because they keep getting sidetracked learning shocking historical truths incomprehensible to mere mortals like you and I, with the power to reshape history as we know it for all history books to come, enacted by the local traveling circus, shadow puppet theater, and hobo acting troupe... in a little segment I like to call... Meanwhile. *hold for studio applause* Meanwhile! It's a panacea for the soul. It'll cure what ails ya. Or ail what cures you, jury's still out on that one. Meanwhile..." *smash cut to the main story*
Damn you, entropy!
Eh? What a fucking BOP!
Me, groovin' and a-boogyin' to the afformentioned bop ^
Then we rest.
I think I'm pretty Sam-ish. Up for long hikes, decent at cooking potatoes, loyal. Suspicious of ghouls. The total package!
My eye is twitching a little at this one. Why body gotta itch in such a way that I gotta bite in order to fix it? Why you doin' this, baby? Why you gotta make me hit you? You know I love you, right? *leg starts twitching* *punch, punch, punch at the twitchy spot* Sometimes, violence is the ONLY solution. Listen, I'm allowed to do violence to my own body. It's the one body I've got absolute autonomy over.
Do with this what you will.
Be the anarchic butthole you want to see in the world.
I would eat breakfast here, and any time anyone walked within view, ask them how they plead.
item.dc:creator: J4nein4ne

item.title: Everyone was thinking it!
item.description: My wife had to work late so I decided to walk to the local trivia night. My wife showed up in round 3….to be clear before so e showed up I hadn’t missed a single question, as soon as she walked in I missed like every question. She’s just the worst.
item.dc:creator: neospor1n

item.title: My Goddess Nyx cosplay from Hades (supergiant games)
item.description: Cosplayer insta @/tinkeringpixie Photographer @/bitterlemonsphotography HADES 2 NEWS HAS ME ABSOLUTELY GEEKED OUT!!! What are you guys excited for??? I watched the technical test and lost my goddamn mind. I’m going to absolutely scream if Nyx shows up in-game with a redesign like the others gods/goddesses got!!!
item.dc:creator: tinkeringpixie

item.dc:creator: ChalupBatmanSugarBottomTheThird

item.title: It was almost worth getting it stolen
item.description: I set my backpack down by my feet at a restaurant and by the time I paid the check, it was gone. Completely stolen. Never experienced anything like this before. I had to take the train to the U.S. embassy - who weren’t open at normal hours (?!) - and met the couple there. They were extremely eccentric. We bonded over this experience though. After we got our emergency passports, they took me out to breakfast and I was telling them about my hostel. They were utterly horrified at the idea of hostels being a thing and said they have good rapport with this ritzy hotel they’re staying at. They ended up getting me a room! Crazy how things work out.
item.dc:creator: zoraniko

item.title: Medicaid said my teeth were fine. I won the second appeal.
item.description:
I didn't have insurance for 10 years. Fell through all the cracks. Suddenly qualified after my husband died (years ago, but thanks) and I started getting more money from the death benefits than we got from his disability. ??? Trying to get my teeth fixed has been a journey. This was back in November.
I have a single bottom molar and the dentures don't fit. Thats mah chewin toof.
Upper crowns
And a partial. Still have to do crowns on the bottom and then dentures will need adjusted, but it's tots doable and then...maybe...I'll have a mouth full of teeth I can actually chew with? What would that be like???
